name: Peanut Brittle
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 1 1/3 liter casserole dish, stir together the sugar and corn syrup. Microwave at high power for 4 minutes.
2. Stir in the peanuts. Continue microwaving at high power for an additional 3-5 minutes until smooth.
3. Add the butter and vanilla extract (our friend didn't specify an exact amount; you will need to experiment with this) – blend well. Microwave at high power for 1-2 more minutes.
4. Add the baking soda, sprinkling it evenly over the top of the mixture. Gently stir until light and foamy. Pour the mixture onto a lightly greased cookie sheet.
5. Allow the brittle to cool for half an hour to one hour before breaking into small pieces. Store in an airtight container.
Difficulty: easy
Precision: approximate measurements
